venue: Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens, USA
h2h_summary: Uruguay leads the head-to-head with 1 win and 1 draw from 2 prior meetings, including a 1-0 win at the 2018 World Cup (Luis Suarez goal) and a 1-1 friendly draw in 2014.

notable_results: 0-1 (Uruguay win) - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 2018

wc_history_home: Saudi Arabia have appeared at seven World Cups, with their best run coming in 1994 (Round of 16), and their most iconic moment being the stunning 2-1 upset of eventual champions Argentina at Qatar 2022.
wc_history_away: Uruguay are one of football's most decorated nations, having won the World Cup twice (1930, 1950) and reached the semi-finals five times, including a fourth-place finish as recently as 2010.
group_context: Group H features pre-tournament favourites Spain alongside Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde; Saudi Arabia desperately need points in their opening match to avoid early elimination, while Uruguay must assert themselves as the second-strongest side in the group to secure a last-16 berth.
strengths_home: Organised, disciplined defensive block difficult to break down
Salem Al-Dawsari's match-winning individual quality on the counter
High-press intensity capable of disrupting structured opponents
strengths_away: Elite midfield trio of Valverde, Ugarte and Bentancur dominating central areas
Experienced and commanding centre-back partnership of Gimenez and Araujo
Darwin Nunez's pace and physicality stretching defences in behind
team_style_home: Saudi Arabia under Georgios Donis deploy a compact 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 with disciplined defensive organisation, aggressive pressing to win possession high up the pitch, and rapid counter-attacks through Al-Dawsari and Al-Buraikan.
team_style_away: Uruguay under Marcelo Bielsa play a high-intensity 4-4-2 or 4-3-3 built on relentless pressing, quick vertical transitions, and an adventurous defensive line with full-backs bombing forward, driven by Valverde's box-to-box engine.
recent_news: Saudi Arabia and Uruguay will both look to start their 2026 World Cup campaigns with a win when they go head-to-head at Hard Rock Stadium on Monday. Uruguay have injury concerns, including Ronald Araujo, who will not take part due to physical problems.
key_players_home: Salem Al-Dawsari (captain/winger, AFC Player of the Year 2025)
context: Group H features pre-tournament favourites Spain alongside Uruguay, Saudi Arabia, and Cape Verde; Saudi Arabia desperately need points in their opening match to avoid early elimination, while Uruguay must assert themselves as the second-strongest side in the group to secure a last-16 berth.
